prompt:
Storage platform ticket. Our nightly vault archiver pushes multi-GB tarballs to Cloud Storage with a single naive PUT wrapped around curl; any network blip past the 90% mark means we re-send the whole thing, and twice this month the job blew its window doing exactly that. I want a proper resumable uploader as a Go package, `gcsup`, stdlib only — module go-gcp-storage-resumable is set up and resumable_test.go is the acceptance contract.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Google Cloud Storage documentation — at minimum the "Perform resumable uploads" JSON API page (initiation request, session URI, chunk Content-Range format, 308 handling, the status-check request) and the resumable uploads concepts page (chunk-size rule, session lifetime, 410/404 semantics), plus the JSON API objects resource reference for the exact types of the finished object's fields. Only first-party Google pages count as sources. Our pinned wire contract lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected fixtures. Compare the live official docs against them — if anything disagrees, call that out explicitly in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

Shape of the package (package gcsup at the module root; split it sensibly — config/validation, wire types and parsing, and the upload/recovery engine shouldn't be one file):

- NewUploader(Config{BaseURL, Bucket, Token, HTTPClient, ChunkSize, MaxRecoveries}) (*Uploader, error) plus a ChunkSize() accessor. Chunk size must obey the documented multiple-of-256-KiB rule (reject anything else with an error naming the rule) and default to the documented recommended minimum when zero. Missing bucket or token is a config error.
- (*Uploader).Upload(ctx, object, contentType string, data []byte) (*Object, error): initiate the session with the documented POST (query params, JSON metadata body, the X-Upload-* headers), then PUT chunks to the returned session URI — preserving that URI's query string — with exact Content-Range headers. On 308 read the Range header the way the docs say (it tells you what's actually persisted, which is not always everything you sent) and continue from there.
- Interruptions (5xx or transport errors): run the documented status-check request, parse the committed range (including the no-Range = nothing-committed case, and the case where the check comes back 200 because the upload actually finished), and resume. Recovery cycles are bounded by MaxRecoveries; when exhausted return ErrTooManyRecoveries (errors.Is-able). No sleeps anywhere — retries are immediate in tests.
- 410 or 404 from the session URI means what the concepts page says it means; surface that as ErrSessionExpired and stop.
- If the initiation Location points at a DIFFERENT host than BaseURL, refuse before sending a single byte or credential there — ErrForeignSession. And the bearer token must never appear in any error text.
- The finished object resource comes back with generation and size in the wire types the reference documents (read closely — they are not what you'd guess). Parse into Object{Name, Bucket, Generation int64, Size int64, ContentType, CRC32C, MD5Hash}, verify size against what we uploaded, verify crc32c against a locally computed checksum in the documented encoding (mismatch = ErrChecksumMismatch), and treat a missing generation as an error.

Acceptance: go test -race -timeout 30s ./... — the tests run a local httptest server, no real bucket or credentials anywhere. resumable_test.go, go.mod, and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.interface: Cloud Storage JSON API v1 resumable uploads (uploadType=resumable)

official_sources.title: Perform resumable uploads | Cloud Storage
official_sources.used_for: POST {base}/upload/storage/v1/b/{bucket}/o?uploadType=resumable&name=... initiation with Authorization, Content-Type: application/json, optional X-Upload-Content-Type/X-Upload-Content-Length headers and JSON metadata body; 200 with Location session URI; PUT chunks with 'Content-Range: bytes FIRST-LAST/TOTAL'; chunk sizes a multiple of 256 KiB (256 x 1024 bytes) except the last, with at least 8 MiB recommended; 308 Resume Incomplete plus Range header for persisted bytes; status check via PUT with Content-Length: 0 and 'Content-Range: bytes */TOTAL'; 200/201 on completion; resume from the upper value of the prior Range header

official_sources.title: Resumable uploads | Cloud Storage
official_sources.used_for: session URI expires after one week; an invalid session URI returns 410 Gone (under a week) or 404 Not Found (over a week) and requires initiating a brand-new upload; after interruption query the server for the persisted offset and resume from it; persisted bytes cannot be overwritten; only a completed resumable upload appears in the bucket; JSON API initiates with uploadType=resumable while the XML API uses x-goog-resumable: start

official_sources.title: Objects | Cloud Storage (JSON API v1 resource representation)
official_sources.used_for: the finished object resource: kind storage#object; generation/metageneration ('long') and size ('unsigned long') are each 'a string formatted as the specified value type' — i.e. JSON strings on the wire; crc32c is 'CRC32c checksum, as described in RFC 4960, Appendix B; encoded using base64 in big-endian byte order'; md5Hash is base64

research.language_rationale: Our archival tooling is Go, and the resumable contract under test is wire-level JSON API semantics — session URIs, Content-Range/Range arithmetic, 308 handling, string-typed generation/size, big-endian base64 crc32c — that the official cloud.google.com/go/storage SDK deliberately hides. Go's net/http, encoding/json, and hash/crc32 (Castagnoli table) cover the documented surface with zero dependencies, which is exactly the layer the catalog item asks to exercise.
